focus: outcome of last week's job interview
cardReadingDirection: Yes — the spread leans clearly toward getting an offer, because all three cards emphasize skillful action, completion, and supportive collaboration around this interview. Card 1 (The Magician) = the user presented their skills convincingly and has real leverage here; they should reinforce that impression by sending a follow-up that concretely restates what they can deliver in the role. Card 2 (The World) = this hiring process is reaching its natural completion in the user's favor, likely closing a cycle such as a job search or career chapter, so they should prepare for offer logistics like salary expectations and start dates rather than more interviews. Card 3 (Three of Swords upright, read here as growth through teamwork) = the deciding factor is how well the panel believes the user will fit the team, so any remaining contact should emphasize collaboration and shared results, not solo achievements. Practical step: today, send a short follow-up email to the interviewer thanking them, naming one team-oriented contribution you would make in your first month, and asking about the timeline for their decision.
